items.title: TIFF spotlights record number of Indigenous films
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The Toronto International Film Festival featured a record number of films made by Indigenous people in Canada this year, a milestone celebrated by Indigenous artists who say the industry has long sidelined their voices. 
 
Reuters journalist Vanessa Balintec sat down with some of them.

items.title: 'Anything to keep my child safe': Polish mother trains for war | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>A few miles from the Russian border in northern Poland, office administrator Agnieszka Jedruszak is digging a trench. Driven by fear of war with Russia, she wants to be able to defend her family, including her 13-year-old son.

items.title: Charlie Kirk, UN vote, NATO-Poland, Chicago and Premier League
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Erika Kirk, widow of conservative activist Charlie Kirk (https://www.reuters.com/world/us/suspect-charlie-kirk-murder-custody-after-33-hour-manhunt-2025-09-12/) , vows his movement will continue after suspect Tyler Robinson is arrested for Kirk’s fatal shooting at a campus event. The UN General Assembly overwhelmingly backs a two-state solution (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/un-overwhelmingly-endorses-two-state-solution-declaration-that-condemns-hamas-2025-09-12/)  for Israel and the Palestinians, drawing sharp criticism from Israel and the U.S. NATO pledges stronger defence of its eastern flank as Poland's army grows. In Chicago, an ICE officer kills a man during an arrest after he allegedly dragged an officer with his car. And the English Premier League (https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/isak-lead-parade-new-signings-premier-league-resumes-2025-09-11/)  returns with record signings. 



Listen to our longform podcast ⁠⁠On Assignment⁠⁠ (https://www.reuters.com/podcasts/on-assignment/)
